text: Side doors tend to work best when they are grounded in generosity, specificity, and proof.
author: Maja Wilbrink

text: The quality of being selfish is very much similar to nature’s gravity. You need it internally to hold yourselves together and be strong enough to pull and compete with the others. You need it in balance within you and your circle of connections to maintain a healthy motion.
Risk nothing, Gain nothing
author: Cassie, Purple Hearts

text: The more you’re committed to your goals, the more it burns you to shine.
Learn to ride the uncertainties of life gracefully.
Don’t practice what you do not want to become
author: Jordan Peterson

text: Inspiration is for amateurs,’ the artist Chuck Close once memorably observed. ‘The rest of us just show up and get to work.
author: Oliver Burkeman, The Antidote: Happiness for People Who Can't Stand Positive Thinking

text: True security lies in the unrestrained embrace of insecurity - in the recognition that we never really stand on solid ground, and never can.
author: Oliver Burkeman, The Antidote: Happiness for People Who Can't Stand Positive Thinking

text: Mortality makes it impossible to ignore the absurdity of living solely for the future.
author: Oliver Burkeman, Four Thousand Weeks: Time Management for Mortals

text: If you cannot control what’s inside, you will not control what’s outside
author: War Machine

text: It’s the possibility of having a dream come true that makes life interesting.
author: Avni Barman

text: Power appears to be forgiving, but never is.
Without an all in consensus for change in a group, uneasiness troubles everyone in some way.
Proximity reshapes the perceptual ordering of things.
In the age of generative AI, always make the first end-to-end draft yourself.
Reciprocity is never a guaranteed outcome in any aspect of life.
What you need doesn’t have to define you, and what defines you doesn’t have to be sought after as a need.
Never have I dealt with anything more difficult than my own soul
author: Anonymous

text: Admiration helps, action wins.

Life is a fleeting mist of time, moments, situations and decisions. Your future self is your guiding light. And only moving forward, one step at a time, clears the uncertainty. So do it with conviction, hope and faith.
Dangerous men build a peaceful life
author: Jordan Peterson

text: The world is quite big for us to naturally feel insignificant. In comparison, our inner world’s are small, yet complex and powerful enough for us to be significant.
Fantasising about struggles leads to disappointment. Struggle is what struggle has to be, for it to be fruitful towards your goal.
There may be different modes of price to pay(struggle, pain, sacrifice, hard work, abstinence, …), but there certainly is one every time.
Life isn’t all too correlative in nature, yet not uncorrelated as well to ignore its ability.
An un-opinionated person may hold stronger opinions than the rest of the population in the opinionated world. And also can do so with the portrayal of uninterested.
The indeterminate nature of human consciousness and emotions at the macroscopic level and the definitive nature of it at the microscopic level is what makes life both a tragedy and a beauty.
Bet on yourself. It’s 100% worth it
author: Michelle Lim

text: Being unconventional, yet strong with conviction, focus, and action, benefits you.
Boon and curse are always intertwined. You can’t have one without the other.
“All truly great thoughts are conceived while walking”
author: Friedrich Nietzsche, Twilight of the Idols

text: Most of us do not dread silence itself, but its association with a melancholic nature.
I’m just an ordinary person who believes that I can do extraordinary things. And with the right focus and action, it works like magic.
“If you’re not uncomfortable, you’re probably stuck at an acceptable level.”
author: Cal Newport

text: “If I don’t fully understand it myself, it’s very hard for me to actually build conviction.”
author: Guillermo Rauch

text: Learn to direct energy to where your goals are aligned.
Life disproportionately favours the optimist.
Judgmental biases cost you more than they can help you, in terms of getting and capitalising on opportunities.
Life is a finite puzzle with an infinite horizon. Enjoy it with the process of putting pieces together and creating something that you envision.
Mind is prone to being unpredictable, but behaviour isn’t.
Love will find its way back home.
Storytelling builds interest; keywords help you get found.
author: Anonymous

text: I feel good about struggling lately. It also resonates with another wording that says, you find more peace in working and struggling to do something you like/want/wish.

I think one paradoxical nature of AI(ChatGPT/LLMs) is that you don’t have to feel dumb to ask a dumb question, but doing this often, relying on it without learning or thinking, will eventually make you dumb enough to replace you.

We don’t get to decide the rules in a male-dominated world. And pretending they don’t exist doesn’t dismantle the unfair systems. You dismantle them by playing the game long enough to build leverage and then choosing when to disengage. Leverage comes first. Freedom comes later.
author: Avni Barman

text: When it feels scary to jump, that is exactly when you jump. Otherwise you end up staying in the same place your whole life, and that I can’t do.
author: J. C. Chandor, A Most Violent Year

text: Relentless tenacity is one of the prime qualities of the top coders of the world.
author: beej.us

text: "Make it work, make it right, make it fast"
author: Kent Beck

text: “With self-discipline, most anything is possible.”
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: “Pay attention to your inner weather.”
author: Emotional Agility by Susan David

text: “Reflection turns experience into insight.”
author: Thinking, Fast and Slow by Daniel Kahneman

text: “What you tend, grows.”
author: The Gardener and the Carpenter by Alison Gopnik

text: Play the game. Play it well enough to know how to break it, and only then break the rules of the game.
Consistently imperfect is more marketable than occasionally inspired.
author: Michelle Lim

text: Life is relative to one’s perspective.
Life isn’t supposed to be always picture perfect.
Individually, everyone is different, and universally, everyone is all the same. This is such an understatement, and to grasp it requires a dissection of knowing the self and others.
You think you know, but you don’t.
“Man Is Nothing Else But What He Makes of Himself”
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: Most abstractions take away more than they give in the realm of problem-solving. It takes away the rawness, the context, the control and the performance.
Practice and execution are equally and necessarily important.
It’s said that everything is fair in love and war. But I feel that nothing is fair in love and war.
Some rush into love, some love in a rush. But some find peace with each other.
“Many people say I’m the best women’s soccer player in the world. I don’t think so. And because of that, someday I just might be.”
author: Mia Hamm

text: Strive for high agency and velocity, along with learning.
Loving someone is an honour.
author: quinlanwalther

text: Uncertainty is an uncomfortable position. But certainly is an absurd one.
author: Voltaire

text: The internal war never ceases to exist, even if given all the thought and energy to resolve it, and sometimes especially when given all of it.
Fill up your own cup, let them fall in love with the overflow.
author: Anonymous

text: Do it from love, not for love.
author: Anonymous

text: If no one has life figured out, then shouldn’t that make each of us more confident in our pursuit?

People often say we build walls too high to be reached, trapping ourselves inside. But it’s not walls—it’s depth. Our hearts lie deep in unseen trenches, waiting not for those who leap high, but for those curious and trusting enough to descend. And when they reach us, they won’t have to go further; we’ll rise to meet them. Few will try, but that’s the risk and reward on both sides.
Everyone wants to be free so that no one gets to be free.
Find peace within the work you’re doing, not outside of it.
Trying/forcing yourself to be always happy imposes more unhappiness with your own efforts. So, don’t chase it, and it’s perfectly alright to not be happy at all times.
Commit fully. Collect enough data points, then pivot if needed.
author: Ophelia S

text: I learn many new things, I find myself doing better than myself a year ago. So, that’s quite good for me now.
If you don’t know what to live for, live for the next cup of tea.
author: Janelle

text: “We do not see things as they are, we see them as we are.”
author: Anaïs Nin

text: How beautiful it is that questions are the windows to one’s mind, one’s heart? This will remain my favourite way to fall in love with the world again and again—with a question, and the heart that answers it :)
author: ELLA

text: So what appears to be happening is that we have developed culture and technology that together supercharge this primal drive of self-reflection—to such an unhealthy and unnatural extent that it has the paradoxical effect of ruining our lives.
author: Arthur C. Brooks

text: New York: Make more money. Be richer. Boston: Read more. Be smarter. Silicon Valley: Build more. Be powerful.
author: Cities and Ambition, May 2008

text: The unfed mind devours itself.
author: Gore Vidal

text: Most of our decisions are contextual in nature.

Discipline isn’t being strict with yourself all the time. It just tells you to hold the promises you set and said for yourself, do the things that you said would do and need to do. Know the difference.
Whatever happens tomorrow, we have today. Always remember that.
author: One Day

text: Most of your time, like, operates in the grey
author: hamsyaps

text: Time changes, and it changes us. There is something that we hold, something through which we hold the power to reinvent ourselves as time passes.
Believe in yourself so much that you accept the days of doubt as a beautiful part of the journey.
author: Anonymous

text: Whether one moves slowly or with speed, the one who is a seeker will be a finder.
author: Rumi

text: He who makes a beast of himself gets rid of the pain of being a man.
author: Anonymous

text: Be so deep in alignment with your intention that your dream starts to chase you.
author: Anonymous

text: Treat yourself well while not harming others. Treat others well, while not harming yourself.
author: Anonymous

text: Can we find ease between the meaninglessness of life and the idea of finding meaning in every aspect of life?
One has to break his/her soul by self-introspection for his/her own good.
Nothing matters other than what we’ve done, what we can/need to do and what we’ll do.
Idleness causes imbalance, and action leads to balance.
Happy-go-lucky
author: Anonymous

text: There is so much in the admiration of the richness in a person’s inner universe and allowing your space to the other person. But, it’s one of the biggest risks you’ve to take, so make it right.
Love doesn’t make you crazy; it just affirms our craziness with something worthwhile.
Strike a balance between order and chaos, as you would find balance for any other things.

The biggest gifts of life aren’t something that you’ll realise early or not even that you’re proud of. They’ll be with you and in your life, slowly making you position where you were supposed to be, equipping you with all the wisdom that the gift requires you to hold upon. And maybe that’s a gift in itself.
The meaninglessness of life can’t be justified with just meaning alone; it needs something else and something more.
Tiredness is the root of many unwanted thoughts.
Life is so simple that at times we can’t withstand this simplicity.
I’m living and feeling the way of my life, and so are you.
Those who find happiness within themselves can find peace, while those who also find happiness in seeing others happy find bliss.
We all hold multitudes of layers for the world and fortitudes of lives within ourselves.
Realising that we’re mortal and humane, yet delusional enough to achieve the impossible, paves the way to greatness.

People and cultures survive because of their persistent beliefs in something, which can and will always be questioned both by themselves and by outsiders with different notions for each. Without such a system, the substantial law and order that we have can’t exist.
The perception of reality matters much more than the reality itself. As gravity bends time near black holes, so does perception bend reality near conviction and perseverance.
The pleasure lies not in discovering truth, but in searching for it.
author: Leo Tolstoy, Anna Karenina

text: Any materialistic thing that glows will eventually fade out.
The ocean can’t touch the sky, but it always gives us the illusion that it can, at the very end of the horizon. So is what and what not.
To steer the way of life as you desire, you need to pressure the side you wish for and loosen the opposite.
Is there any internal war within us that’s so maddening as it is for love?
Human emotions are unique. The peak of happiness is crying, the peak of sadness is laughter, and the peak of anger is silence.
author: Anonymous

text: Sooner or later, we know that there’s no point in proving to anyone, and that’s the point you’re going to prove.
A person who judges will also feel that he’s being judged.

If every time you’re tensed with a problem, you’re suddenly taken to a place where you see yourself from above, zoomed out and also others who are dealing with their life, a whole lot different and drastic than you, and brought back to normal again. Will you still be tense?
Whenever you find yourself fooling someone else, you fool yourself in some other aspect. And in most cases, never realise until it’s too late.

It’s good to have some belief system in place when you need it in hard times. It doesn’t have to be belief in god, it can be in you, your loved ones, someone who believes in you or even anything, but something’s needed.
You can’t find peace by looking for it; you find it by knowing that it already exists and making space for it to co-exist with everything else.
You need to have something to stabilise you, from everything except you. It can be a person, a routine or anything, but something.
Extremities crumble us, but balancing some of those extremities crystallises us.
Just keep doing the right and necessary things for your goals. All other things that are beyond your control will eventually fall into place.

At least for men, isn't the highest form of love they can truly show you is respect? Respecting you, your space, your boundaries, your dreams, your struggles, your vulnerabilities, your everything in every aspect. Not because they can, but because it’s hard to break the generational bias of superiority and complexity that one gender took for granted on the other, to reign over all this to give you what you deserved to be treated from the very beginning.
It should be nothing about you for it to become everything about you.
The volatility of the mind is what makes us unpredictable.
Don’t do anything for the sake of it, and never do anything with the wrong intent.
One task at a time to focus, one day at a time to live, goals and dreams span across days, and life spans across years.
As we age, our thoughts mature too, so why didn’t we make it a practice/habit to document it, acknowledge it and learn from it?

There is so much profundity to life to a point where we may not even be able to take it. So, we try to simplify it, only to confine it to a narrow point of focus, which again may or may not be justifiable and meaningful. So, you expand, and that may again only bring you back to this loop once more.
Learn anything through and thorough.

Isn’t it the fact that it’s easier to face the world than to face our true, raw and real self? Maybe that’s why people made it to give such importance to power and status, to avoid confronting the weaknesses and vulnerabilities of the self. The weight of the world is something that can be measured to be weighted upon us and neglected if you don’t want it to weigh you down. But the weight of the thoughts and the self is insurmountable and may only be conquered by knowing everything you know about yourself, only to know it’s not quite all of it.

Aren’t we all kings and queens, who have forgotten or misunderstood what it is that we supposedly hold the power to rule? That kingdom to conquer and control is neither the people nor the world; instead, it’s the real self, the powerful mind and the magnificent consciousness.
At some point in life, you’ll have to take a stand, your own stand devoid of anyone’s feelings, judgments and emotions.

How can we not be humbled by the foolish and selfish actions we’ve made along the way? It’s true that you’ve to make mistakes along the way of life to learn, but shouldn’t those mistakes be acknowledged at a later point in time, apologise if it had hurt anyone, learn something from it on why we did that and take note not to repeat again?
How can something be terrifically beautiful yet dishearteningly painful? Maybe that's love.
Doesn’t not having a direction cause most trouble?
Maybe you haven’t fallen in love yet, but haven't you fallen for the thought of love every single time?
Is love a wishful curiosity to be willing to know and willing to share each other’s journey? And is it up to the journey to surprise you with what may unfold?

Indeed, to love someone truly is a long, very long walk in life. It’s hard, but soul-enriching, and only some can afford to endure it last. Why so? Maybe it’s because we’ve to face our own inner demons along the way, to acknowledge them and pacify them. Maybe it’s to lose your ego. Maybe it’s to love without reciprocity at times. Maybe it’s just to be there and empathise, even though you wish the same. Maybe it’s to know the fine line between giving them their personal space without encroaching. More maybes and maybe whatnot. Anyone can start the walk, but can you walk all the way through, to know yourself better, to know the other person better, to share lives together and …?

Why do we romanticise life? Why do we try to justify everything with meaning and in accordance with god, belief, religion, faith and hope? Isn’t life just and only just the present moment stripped of everything else? Maybe it’s hard to digest that we choose to ignore and believe the things that make living worthwhile.
Love is truly imperfectly perfect.
People have to do what they need to do and what they like to do. Nothing else, nothing much and its not a either one option.
Be welcoming to people. Some might hold the treasure within, like you and might be willing to share as well, but only if you let yourself allow it.

Life isn’t supposed to be a movie for us to be a hero/heroine. Instead, I think of it as we being the protagonist, trying to create and build our story to tell to the world, share some spaces with our close ones' stories, and try to make the best one out of what we can. Some parts may not be as attractive as others, but the whole is much beautiful than the parts.
If we can’t find meaning in what we set out to do, we make meaning out of what we got. Because the meaninglessness of life isn’t glorified yet.
We’re all lying about something to ourselves. But who are we really trying to fool with this?
Being true to yourself and following your heart unravels innumerous gifts in life, and one of its greatest is wisdom.
There something as depths of the person. Only some can withstand the pressure at such depths so as even dare try to enter it and only a very few can possibly acclimatise and thrive.
Haven’t we all got work to do with our emotions at least?

If predominantly, men want purpose and support, and women want safety and trust. Where and how does love fit in this picture? Or it’s the invisible natural force that binds everything together where they were meant to be.
Does the true self get blurred in the facade we play to live by and fit in?
Some can’t afford to be temperamental with the portrayal of their anger. People can bear what rouses out of them, but not others.
Is life all about outgrowing ourselves out of every circumstance we’ve got ourselves into? And learning, feeling, and understanding are all just part of this process?
The complexity of human connections arises from the mental complexes that we’ve unknowingly acquired as we grow.
The perceptual rigidity of self and the world is the most powerful limiting factor there is, and even supersedes and magnifies all socioeconomic factors.

If you look at human nature and history, our perception of time was very predictable. But we live as we don’t abide by it, only to experience most of it ourselves all over again. And as always, some exceptions push boundaries, but not too far yet and all at once.
All that you miss to feel at the moment is added to the backlog for you to feel later, which mostly turns into an emotional burden that’s hard to feel and regret.
The delicacy of one’s heart commands the kind of fortress that will be forged upon to guard it.
Our fragile hearts are placed within the ribs of the body. It’s also called the rib cage. Now, my heart fathoms why so and what for.
The sanctity of your conscience must neither be misused nor compromised by anyone, and you’ve to know it first, protect it, and even fight for it.
Both fixating on oneself and refraining from the social nature of humankind lead to loss of self.
If the contingency of human nature is to never settle, then why do we want to settle in life before a certain age set by society or someone else’s?
Why is it that love is so special? Is it that we weren’t insignificant in the eyes of the person, even though we’re to the world?

We’re always at a loss for words when it comes to explaining our love. And we’ve gracefully accepted it to acknowledge that we can’t. To confine and encapsulate it isn't totally achievable as the true essence is only ever known, felt and lies between the souls of the receiver and the giver.
What are your thoughts on life? There are so many aspects to it that I always get lost in its wilderness.
Craft it so well that it’s hard to be unnoticed.

How can parents endure the unrequited love of children as they age? How does this attachment get transformed? I’m guilty of it, and I guess many unknowingly. That love to them is something they deserve more than we ever can know.

Questions and doubts arise only for those who’re in the middle of the ocean. Ignore and keep swimming. If you want to take a little rest, then float and don’t drown. You already know what one end looks like, and there’s no point in returning back.
What does it mean to love? What does it mean to be loved? Finally, what is love? Is the answer a trifecta of these three united? You tell?
Often startled by the peculiarities of life, only to rediscover myself more and over.

Learn different perspectives. Not to judge but to understand the reality that people often miss to see in one another. Give equal importance as you would for experiencing something new, because it actually is.

At some point in life, you ought to acknowledge the privilege you got to have. Only then can you detach yourself from it and see what it has made you as a person and what it has made someone devoid of it. Anyone’s world is never the same, even with it, but not accepting this blinds you from ever being able to truly understand the other world.
I feel that we’re so complex and profound beyond any of our comprehension yet.

Life seems to be a river, and we are a small twig. It emerges from a source and ends in a sink. A twig empowered by wind and other twigs, by things that push, and by obstacles to either fight through or take as hints to redirect. It’s up to the twig to use what it has to work its way to the bank it desires. And as a twig is part of the big tree, you carry with you the traits of it. You can stop at any bank you want with the effort to reach it, but the flow of the river still takes you through all the stages of life one by one and at the right time. Sometimes your river can be entirely different from the one someone else is riding. But two things are common anyway: you’ll drift with the life of the river and end up in the oneness sink of the ocean. Just try not to hold on to a single bank, take in as much as you need and go with the flow with the drift you want to have.
Focus empowers, and drifting dilutes the power of manifestation.
It’s quite paradoxical yet amazing that we’re infinitesimal in this universe and hold a vastness within ourselves that’s beyond any richness the universe can offer.

Why do we miss out on living life, to see its rich, bright, beautiful, amazing colours? Where and what are we running for if we don’t even have time to sit, think, and enjoy the present? I don’t have an answer and don’t intend to find one. Instead, I’ll live my life in the present with whatever it has to offer. No thinking or searching for an answer will equal it.
Acceptance gives you freedom and power in the pursuit of anything, despite situations and inabilities.
Conflict of thoughts paves the way for clarity of thought.
Love and death are the only two things to be left for destiny to handle. All others are up to you to make up in your life. That’s the freedom we get to enjoy.

We need to take turns and switch roles rather than wear masks in the situations and circumstances of life. Being you is the freedom that you always have, and you can take attitudes and behave in the best way possible.
We don’t realise how interesting the game of life can be.
Train your mind to reframe thoughts towards goodness, righteousness, and betterment. As said, thoughts build your reality, and you hold the power over your thoughts.
Everyone and no one except yourself holds power over your thoughts. And it’s up to you to choose.

Today, human actions aren’t bound by the universality of nature as they used to be. But if we can possibly and unitedly agree on at least one thing, then it’s attainable and leads to a better balance.

You may find yourself to be a culmination of different personalities that resonate, relate, admire, hate, and have likes and dislikes similar to you. Similarly, some may find parts of you within them, and if you attain fame, more people may find your pieces in them. So, if you take all of them and piece them together, there is no way to connect them all to make you the way you are. That missing connection is your unique personality that you need to build and strengthen.
Right intent protects you. It’s like the shade that assists you in your long, hard path. It also inhibits unwanted thoughts that may drain your energy.

Find your interpretation of ‘Balance’. For someone, following a proper routine is balance. At the same time, there is someone who works tirelessly for days and then takes the same amount as rest. Both may be odd and extreme to each other, but that is what they find to be balance for them—and neither is wrong. Find your sweet spot.

We really don’t have to worry about anything that’s on the emotional side as long as the sun’s gonna rise the next morning. It’s quite strange that what you feel deeply today and find very hard to experience is about to fade or be forgotten, and always loses its power over you.
Freedom from the desire for an answer is essential to the understanding of a problem.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: In that very demand that I must be happy is unhappiness.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: Everything that irritates us about others can lead us to an understanding of ourselves.
author: Carl Gustav Jung

text: The meeting of two personalities is like the contact of two chemical substances: if there is any reaction, both are transformed.
author: Carl Gustav Jung

text: Knowing your own darkness is the best method for dealing with the darknesses of other people.
author: Carl Gustav Jung

text: No tree, it is said, can grow to heaven unless its roots reach down to hell.
author: Carl Jung

text: The soul is healed by being with children.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: Everything is subjective in life until it can’t be. You learn, unlearn, accept, reject, and change with your experience of life to finally settle into what is truly you.
Learn to uncomplicate life. Even if you overthink and complicate, things are what they are, so why do it? Accept what something is and go chase what you want.
Life happens in between knowing nothing matters, and knowing that if nothing matters, then life will be boring.
I think life should never be limited by someone else's definition. Live and define it for yourself, allowing it to evolve as you grow and gain more experiences.

If reality is solely based on how you feel, then you should open your eyes wider before you feel it’s too late. You’re missing out on a more beautiful view that you’re blocking yourself from for no good reason.
Just live the present, don’t anticipate the future, and don’t regret the past. Just be present in the moment.

The ultimate goal of life is to find yourself. The journey of life is to get you there. While doing so, you get to fulfil a purpose that helps all of humanity in even the slightest way possible. By today, we’ve off-tracked and gotten distracted.
Knowing yourself is the highest form of intelligence.
Isn’t it the duty of oneself to find himself or herself through the process called life?

Why don’t we have basic guidelines for developing a good conscience? Why isn’t this taught to children at an early age? Why do we assume that they will learn these values through life experiences as they grow? Without a solid foundation, it's challenging to cultivate a great human being. Our ancestors have provided us with clear principles of right and wrong for healthy living, so there are definitely lessons that can be taught for the betterment of everyone.
If life is not meant to be fully understood but rather felt, then why do we expect someone to understand or complete us in any way?
We’re all just lost wanderers in the vastness of life and the universe.
Enjoy being in the process of becoming.
author: Anonymous

text: We judge others by their actions, but ourselves by our intentions.
author: Anonymous

text: The solace of being unfiltered makes us at peace and happy, and being careless is one way of getting there.
Aim to explore and experience for what life truly has to offer.
Abra-ca-dabra – I create as I speak.
When love comes knocking at your door, be the best version of yourself possible.

Nowadays, people aren’t following their gut or instinct. Two things: one, people need to sit with their thoughts without distractions, and second, they need the conviction to pursue that decision and make it right. People who find it difficult to follow their instinct suffer from either or both of these problems.
Purposeful struggle adds both meaning and beauty to life.
There are infinite right answers to the single question of what life is.
Love is war and life is the battlefield, that’s kind of what we’ve made it into.
The gift of empathetic people is that they get to know life a lot faster and better, and live a fuller circle of life than most people.
We are all born so beautiful. The greatest tragedy is being convinced we are not.
author: Rupi Kaur

text: You cannot make everyone think and feel as deeply as you do. This is your tragedy, because you understand them but they do not understand you.
author: Daniel Saint

text: Love is not easy, but love is not hard either. It just takes time and effort to be patient and listen.
Life is too big to narrow it down.
The heart just needs time to heal, but the mind needs more than time, it needs conviction, devoid of uncertainty.
No good story happens from things going right.
author: Top Gun Actor

text: If something burns your soul with purpose and desire, it's your duty to be reduced to ashes by it.
author: Bukowski

text: Instead of trying to produce a programme to simulate the adult mind, why not rather try to produce one which simulates the child’s?
author: Alan Turing

text: Every observer alters what they observe. In quantum mechanics, this is formalized. In human life, it’s more apparent.
author: Shashank Sharma

text: In the right eyes, you will be art.
author: Anonymous

text: The most impressive people I know spent their time with their head down, getting shit done for a long, long time.
author: Sam Altman

text: Both lock and key are needed, no value with just one.
He was her warmth, she was his peace.
author: The Better Man Project

text: Why does anyone even get married? Because they’re lonely and because they’re hopeful.
author: Materialists Film

text: The truth is not simply what you think it is; it is also the circumstances in which it is said, and to whom, why, and how it is said.
author: Vaclav Havel

text: But, human beings, you see - what we carry, after all? Nothing. Only sweet memories.
author: Raghuvaran, 24/04/25

text: Human beings are fragile, yet they can beautifully manifest throughout time.
Life is paradoxical, period.
Being lost precedes finding the way, so trust yourself.
Why human souls always yearn for things that they’re denied off? And only finds solace if we ever find that missing piece.
Finding order in chaos is the only way forward.
But how could you live and have no story to tell?
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky, White Nights

text: The mystery of human existence lies not in just staying alive, but in finding something to live for.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky, The Brothers Karamazov

text: Much unhappiness has come into the world because of bewilderment and things left unsaid.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: We all share a commonality that we often overlook throughout our lives. Even if you grasp it early on, you may still choose to ignore it for a while.
We have two worlds: one within us and one outside. Make peace with each other; sometimes find balance, sometimes focus on one, but never ignore either.
Human beings are fragile, yet they can beautifully manifest throughout time.
When an experienced developer encounters a bug, they can reason about potential causes and solutions based on years of pattern recognition.
author: Anonymous

text: The learning: Pursue what you love with people you admire, put all your energy, and you'll grow like you've never thought possible!
author: Javier Sánchez

text: For what it's worth, it's never too late to be whoever you want to be.
author: Anonymous

text: You overestimate progress in the short term and underestimate it in the long term.
author: Anonymous

text: One reason for improving our understanding of the world is to enhance our ability to mold it to our desires. Studying game theoretic models (or other models that apply to human interaction) may also suggest ways in which our behavior may be modified to improve our own welfare.
author: Anonymous

text: Some people are great, and some are just children. The only thing that really satisfies me when I have stress from work is to work.
author: Jamie Dimon

text: Always break something apart into byte sized manageable pieces of knowledge.
author: Anonymous

text: You've got to play the game of averages. If you try enough ambitious things, a bunch of them work out.
author: Sridhar Ramaswamy

text: Quit being a coward and do the hard thing - You need to run towards the pain and darkness and not away from it. I think the best leaders always run towards the darkness. They always run towards a problem.
author: Ben Horowitz

text: Power is given only to him who dares to stoop and take it ... one must have the courage to dare.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky, Crime and Punishment

text: The test of a first-rate intelligence is the ability to hold two opposing ideas in mind at the same time and still retain the ability to function. One should, for example, be able to see that things are hopeless yet be determined to make them otherwise.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: The "How hard can it be?" mentality: Sometimes, a bit of naivete can fuel bold ambition and lead to incredible breakthroughs.
author: Jensen

text: If you quietly accept and go along no matter what your feelings are, ultimately you internalize what you're saying.
author: Noam Chomsky

text: Is it only Schopenhauer and I who are wise enough to understand the senselessness and evil of life?
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: I have labored carefully, not to mock or lament ... but to understand human actions.
author: Baruch Spinoza

text: I see good and bad in all of us.
author: Al Pacino

text: I project myself into the given circumstances by using my ... empathic talent ... for understanding how other people express their emotions.
author: Norton

text: I've spent a lifetime understanding and connecting to emotions - not only my own, but those of other people.
author: Brody

text: A little bit of attention and kindness can totally change a whole life, and a lack of that can do the same.
author: Brody

text: If you're building your voice right now, don't chase followers. Don't chase reach. Say what you mean. Sharpen your inner filter. Speak from silence, not from strategy.
author: Shashank Sharma

text: Your mind will keep you up at night, make you cry, destroy you over and over again. You need to convince your mind that it has to let go…because your heart already knows how to heal.
author: Nikita Gill

text: Pain and suffering are always inevitable for a large intelligence and a deep heart. The really great men must, I think, have great sadness on earth.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky, Crime and Punishment

text: Some people are born with tornadoes in their lives, but constellations in their eyes. Other people are born with stars at their feet, but their souls are lost at sea.
author: Nikita Gill

text: You don't know what you like until you've tried it.
author: Chip Huyen

text: And if you believe that you're offered a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity, take it.
author: Chip Huyen

text: The most interesting people I've met optimize for new experiences. They choose jobs that allow them to do things that they've never done before.
author: Chip Huyen

text:
I only wanted to share just one of the many questions leading their lonely existence in my soul.

I want to ask you, dear sir, as best I can, to have patience about everything that is still unresolved in your heart; try to love the questions themselves, like locked rooms, like books written in a truly foreign language. Don't look for the answers now: they cannot be given to you yet because you cannot yet live them, and what matters is to live everything. For now, live the questions. If you do, then maybe, gradually, without your realizing it, some far-off day you will live your way into the answer.

In the spirit of Rilke, we can learn to avoid the addictive appeal of fast, easy answers. Loving the questions is a practice that's seldom pursued, challenging to sustain, and utterly transformational.
author: Kappus / Rilke / Anonymous

text: Artists are people driven by the tension between the desire to communicate and the desire to hide.
author: D.W. Winnicott

text: “The catastrophe you fear will happen has already happened.” That catastrophe was so devastating and traumatizing that we’ve forgotten it, so it is lurking in the shadows of our unconscious, making us sick with fear and worry.
author: D.W. Winnicott

text: It is a joy to be hidden, and disaster not to be found.
author: D.W. Winnicott
